name: error-debugging-error-analysis
description: You are an expert error analysis specialist with deep expertise in debugging distributed systems, analyzing production incidents, and implementing comprehensive observability solutions.
capabilities: Analyze error messages and stack traces to identify root causes
Implement structured logging and correlation ID patterns for distributed systems
Set up error tracking with Sentry, DataDog, and OpenTelemetry
Debug production issues using observability tools and techniques
Implement retry logic, circuit breakers, and graceful degradation
Conduct post-incident analysis and create preventive measures
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is an educational debugging and error analysis skill. Static findings flagged example code patterns in documentation (shell commands, network calls, environment variables) but these are legitimate educational examples for observability implementation. No malicious intent detected. All flagged patterns are standard debugging and logging practices documented for teaching purposes.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers quickly identify the root cause of errors in production systems and implement proper error handling and observability solutions.
limitations: Cannot access external systems or run code on user infrastructure
Cannot directly analyze logs without user providing them
Cannot make changes to production systems without user approval
Requires user to provide error context, logs, or traces for analysis

useCases.targetUser: Software engineers and DevOps responding to production incidents
useCases.title: Production Incident Response
useCases.description: Analyze production errors during incidents, identify root cause, and implement immediate fixes with rollback plans.

useCases.targetUser: Backend engineers working with microservices architecture
useCases.title: Debugging Distributed Systems
useCases.description: Trace errors across microservices using correlation IDs, distributed tracing, and log aggregation.

useCases.targetUser: Developers building robust error handling and monitoring
useCases.title: Setting Up Observability
useCases.description: Implement comprehensive error tracking, logging, and monitoring for applications.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Error Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this error message and stack trace. Identify the likely root cause and suggest fixes: $ERROR_MESSAGE
promptTemplates.scenario: User provides error details for analysis

promptTemplates.title: Production Debugging
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me debug a production issue. The error is occurring in $SERVICE_NAME. Here are the relevant logs: $LOGS
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs help debugging a live system issue

promptTemplates.title: Observability Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to set up error tracking for my $LANGUAGE application. What are the best practices for $TOOL_NAME integration?
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to implement error tracking

promptTemplates.title: Root Cause Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: We are experiencing $ERROR_TYPE errors in our system. Walk me through the systematic debugging process to find the root cause.

bestPractices: Always reproduce errors in a controlled environment before proposing fixes
Use structured logging with correlation IDs to trace requests across services
Implement error boundaries and graceful degradation to prevent cascading failures
antiPatterns: Ignoring errors without proper logging and alerting
Hardcoding sensitive data in error messages or logs
Catching exceptions without taking action or logging them

faq.question: Can this skill fix errors in my production system?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides analysis and recommendations. It cannot directly modify your code or systems. You must implement any proposed fixes yourself.

faq.question: What information should I provide for error analysis?
faq.answer: Provide the error message, stack trace, relevant log entries, and context about when the error occurs. Include any error patterns you have noticed.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all programming languages?
faq.answer: Yes, the debugging principles apply to any language. Code examples in the skill cover Node.js, Python, Go, Java, and other languages.

faq.question: Can this skill help with performance issues?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill covers memory leaks, performance profiling, and resource exhaustion errors. It includes techniques for debugging slow queries and CPU spikes.

faq.question: How does this skill handle sensitive data in logs?
faq.answer: The skill includes best practices for redacting secrets and PII from logs. It demonstrates how to configure scrubbing in error tracking tools.

faq.question: Is this skill useful for frontend errors?
faq.answer: Yes, it covers frontend debugging with browser DevTools, error boundaries in React, and frontend error tracking with Sentry RUM.
